editableNewDialogue.text: I’m trying to make a short gig title that isn’t cheesy. Any ideas that sound punchy but still professional?
A good formula is: action + outcome + deliverable. Keep it specific and avoid superlatives. Want it to emphasize “stand out,” “industry format,” or a specific genre?
Emphasize “stand out,” but keep it simple. Also, what’s the character limit again?

Fiverr gig titles cap at 80 characters. Here are a few that “pop” without trying too hard:
- I will ghostwrite a screenplay that stands out
- I will write a standout screenplay in proper industry format
- I will turn your concept into a standout screenplay
- I will elevate your story into a standout screenplay
- I will ghostwrite a sharp, standout feature screenplay
Give me 5 more that feel a little more modern.

Sure—modern, clean, and still clear:
- I will write a screenplay that cuts through the noise
- I will ghostwrite a screenplay audiences remember
- I will craft a screenplay with a strong hook and structure
- I will write a tight, cinematic screenplay from your idea
- I will ghostwrite a bold, high-concept screenplay
Can you tailor them for comedy/sci-fi/horror without listing all the genres?

Yep—genre-flavored without the genre-dump:
- I will ghostwrite a smart, high-concept screenplay
- I will write a darkly fun, cinematic screenplay
- I will craft a tense, twisty screenplay that stands out
- I will ghostwrite a bold script with sharp tone and pace
- I will write a crowd-pleasing screenplay with a killer hook
If the title can hint at my other lane too, cool—half my gigs lately are basically “turn this book into a script” or “make this script read like a novel,” so a little adaptation vibe wouldn’t hurt.

Got you—so you want “stand out” + a subtle adaptation angle, without sounding overhyped. A few punchy options:

- I will make your screenplay stand out (ghostwritten in pro format)
- I will ghostwrite a standout screenplay that’s ready to pitch
- I will craft a screenplay that rises above the usual
- I will ghostwrite a screenplay that actually gets noticed
- I will adapt your book into a screenplay that pops
- I will turn your novel into a film-ready screenplay that stands out

Want it to lean more “book-to-screen” or more “script doctor / rewrite”?
Quick correction before you lock this in: I don’t really “specialize” in love drama. I *can* write drama, but my lanes are comedy, sci-fi, and horror.
Got it. So we’ll position you as specializing in comedy, sci-fi, and horror, and we’ll mention drama as something you can write when the story calls for it—not a core specialty.
Exactly—drama’s the seasoning, not the whole meal.
